content:
First we observe that for any cubic 
\[
p(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx+d,
\]
the symmetry \(x\mapsto 30-x\) gives a convenient relation.  In fact one checks

\[
p(t)+p(30-t)
=\;a\bigl(t^3+(30-t)^3\bigr)
+b\bigl(t^2+(30-t)^2\bigr)
+c\bigl(t+(30-t)\bigr)+2d
\]
is a quadratic in \(t\).  Plugging in \(t=5\) yields the given condition
\[
p(5)+p(25)=1906
\]
and plugging in \(t=15\) gives \(2\,p(15)\).  Subtracting these two equations eliminates \(c\) and \(d\), and one finds
\[
p(15)\;=\;953 \;-\;100\,(b+45a).
\]
Since \(a,b\) are integers, \(p(15)\equiv 953\pmod{100}\), i.e.\ 
\[
p(15)=953-100k,\quad k\in\Bbb Z.
\]
The distances \(|953-100k|\) from zero are minimized when \(100k\) is the nearest multiple of 100 to 953, namely 1000.  Thus the smallest possible \(\bigl|p(15)\bigr|\) is
\[
|953-1000|=47.
\]
One checks easily that one can attain \(\;p(15)=-47\) (for instance take \(a=0\,,\,b=10\,,\,c=0\,,\,d=-2297\)).  Hence the answer is

\[
\boxed{47}.
\]

First we observe that for any cubic 
\[
p(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx+d,
\]
the symmetry \(x\mapsto 30-x\) gives a convenient relation.  In fact one checks

\[
p(t)+p(30-t)
=\;a\bigl(t^3+(30-t)^3\bigr)
+b\bigl(t^2+(30-t)^2\bigr)
+c\bigl(t+(30-t)\bigr)+2d

\[
p(5)+p(25)=1906

and plugging in \(t=15\) gives \(2\,p(15)\).  Subtracting these two equations eliminates \(c\) and \(d\), and one finds
\[
p(15)\;=\;953 \;-\;100\,(b+45a).
\]
Since \(a,b\) are integers, \(p(15)\equiv 953\pmod{100}\), i.e.\ 
\[
p(15)=953-100k,\quad k\in\Bbb Z.
\]
The distances \(|953-100k|\) from zero are minimized when \(100k\) is the nearest multiple of 100 to 953, namely 1000.  Thus the smallest possible \(\bigl|p(15)\bigr|\) is
\[
|953-1000|=47.
\]
One checks easily that one can attain \(\;p(15)=-47\) (for instance take \(a=0\,,\,b=10\,,\,c=0\,,\,d=-2297\)).  Hence the answer is

\[

\]
